title: Swartzia parvipetala
intro: Swartzia parvipetala is a species of flowering plant in the family Leguminosae, native to tropical Africa and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Swartzia parvipetala include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or made into a powder to be used in capsules or topical creams, and is commonly used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Swartzia parvipetala is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Swartzia. The genus Swartzia itself is part of the tribe Detarieae and the subtribe Amherstieae. Swartzia parvipetala is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the Fabaceae family, one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world.
